classObjective: Express a relation as a table, a graph, a mapping, and a set of ordered pairs and identify the domain and range.
workedExamples: 3-1, Example 1 — Multiple Representations of a Relation (Table, Graph, Mapping, Ordered Pairs)
3-1, Example 2 — Identify Independent and Dependent Variables from Graphs
3-1, Example 3 — Graph Data from a Table
warmUp: List 3 examples of real-world relationships that could be represented as ordered pairs
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: four representations of relations; domain and range as sets
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice representing relations in multiple forms and identifying domain/range
independentPractice: Practice problems expressing relations as tables, graphs, mappings, and ordered pairs
assessment: Exit ticket: given a relation as ordered pairs, express it as a table, graph, and mapping, then identify domain and range
capReflection: Why is it useful to represent the same relationship in multiple forms?
